seo.title: AI Agent Error Handling Patterns in Haystack Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent error handling patterns in Haystack agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Haystack pipelines, AI Agent Error Handling Patterns manifest as silent failures in composable components, where type mismatches, irrelevant retrievals, hallucinations, and custom errors disrupt agent reliability without clear feedback. These issues arise from Haystack's modular design, where components like Retrievers and Generators process data asynchronously without built-in validation. Without proper error boundaries, pipelines fail unpredictably in production, leading to degraded agent performance and untraceable bugs.

howItManifests.symptom: Retriever returns list of dicts but Generator expects Document objects, causing AttributeError deep in pipeline execution
howItManifests.impact: Pipeline crashes mid-run with cryptic stack traces, halting entire agent workflow

howItManifests.symptom: Generator receives empty or low-score documents but proceeds without validation, producing generic responses
howItManifests.impact: Agent outputs unhelpful answers that erode user trust without triggering alerts

howItManifests.symptom: Output contains facts not present in input context but no mechanism flags hallucination
howItManifests.impact: Agent delivers confidently wrong information, damaging credibility in RAG applications

howItManifests.symptom: Custom node raises exception but pipeline continues with None values downstream
howItManifests.impact: Partial failures cascade, producing corrupted outputs without surfacing root cause

detection.name: Type Mismatch Monitor
detection.description: Detects output schema violations between consecutive pipeline components using runtime type checking
detection.code:
from haystack import Pipeline
from haystack.components.retrievers.in_memory import InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever
from typing import Any

class TypeMismatchDetector:
    def __init__(self):
        self.prev_schema = None

    def run(self, inputs: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
        current_schema = {k: type(v).__name__ for k, v in inputs.items()}
        if self.prev_schema and self.prev_schema != current_schema:
            raise ValueError(f'Type mismatch: {self.prev_schema} -> {current_schema}')
        self.prev_schema = current_schema
        return inputs

# Usage in pipeline
pipe = Pipeline()
pipe.add_component('type_detector', TypeMismatchDetector())
pipe.connect('retriever.documents', 'type_detector')
pipe.connect('type_detector', 'generator')

fixes.name: Error Boundary Component
fixes.description: Catches and handles errors from downstream components with fallback responses

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Error Handling Patterns Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as a custom Haystack component that automatically monitors all four common failure modes in real-time. It instruments your existing pipeline without code changes, capturing type mismatches, relevance scores, hallucination signals, and error traces. Vex uses statistical baselines from your production traffic to detect anomalies and triggers automated rollbacks or fallbacks.

The Vex component wraps your pipeline endpoints, providing observability dashboards and auto-remediation workflows. When issues are detected, Vex can dynamically reroute traffic to fallback pipelines or healthy component variants. This ensures 99.9% agent uptime while continuously learning from failure patterns.

Installation is simple: pip install haystack-ai vex-sdk, then add the VexMonitor component to any pipeline. Vex handles the complexity of error pattern detection, leaving your team to focus on agent logic rather than defensive programming.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk.haystack import VexMonitor
from haystack import Pipeline

# Install: pip install haystack-ai vex-sdk

pipe = Pipeline()

# Add Vex monitor to existing pipeline
pipe.add_component(
    "vex_monitor",
    VexMonitor(
        project_id="your-project-id",
        patterns=[
            "pipeline-type-mismatch",
            "retriever-irrelevant",
            "generator-hallucination",
            "custom-component-error"
        ]
    )
)

# Wrap existing components
pipe.connect("query", "vex_monitor.query")
pipe.connect("retriever.documents", "vex_monitor.documents")
pipe.connect("vex_monitor.validated", "generator.documents")
pipe.connect("generator.text", "vex_monitor.text")

# Vex auto-generates detection rules and remediation flows
pipe.draw('pipeline_with_vex.png')

# Run with monitoring
result = pipe.run({"query": {"query": "What is Haystack?"}})

# Vex dashboard shows: 98% relevance, 0.2% hallucination rate, 100% type safety

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add Vex to your Haystack pipelines in 5 minutes and eliminate agent error patterns forever: pip install haystack-ai vex-sdk
